betterquesting:10.desc:8:
§6ME Fluid Terminal§f

§aFluid storage access on the ME network.§f

The §bME Fluid Terminal§f provides access to the ME network's fluid storage. View, insert, and extract fluids from the network.

§6Specifications§f:
§b•§f Channels: §e1§f
§b•§f Functions: view, insert, extract fluids
§b•§f Compatible with §bfluid storage cells§f and §bME Fluid Storage§f disks
§b•§f Works with all fluid containers

§6How to Use§f:
§b•§f Right-click with a fluid container (bucket, cell, fluid capsule) to deposit or withdraw fluids
§b•§f The terminal displays all stored fluids with quantities
§b•§f Search bar filters by fluid name
§b•§f Shift-click to move full containers

§6Crafting Recipe§f:
§b•§f §b1× ME Terminal§f
§b•§f §b1× Illuminated Panel§f
§b•§f §b1× Formation Core§f
§b•§f §b1× Iron Ingot§f

§6Strategic Role§f:
The §bME Fluid Terminal§f is the §9primary fluid interface§f for AE2. Pair with §bME Fluid Interface§f and §bFluid Storage Cells§f for full fluid automation.

§6Why It Matters§f:
Required for any automation chain that handles fluids. Common pattern: §bWater source → ME Fluid Interface → network → ME Fluid Interface → destination machine§f.

§eTip§f: Place an §bME Fluid Terminal§f next to your §bwater source§f for infinite water import automation.
